extraction.one_line_summary: CORENGO Eros won stage 3 after recovering from poor positioning before the final climb, surging past VILELA and KWIATKOWSKI in final 200m.
tactical_patterns: VILELA Andrea attacked on final climb approach but faded after leading into final turn, suggesting punch insufficient for sub-300m sprints.
CORENGO used climbing ability to recover positioning lost before finale rather than sitting in, revealing hybrid climber-puncher tactical advantage.
ROGLIC Primoz lost yellow jersey margin from 3+ minutes to 6 seconds, exposing Jumbo vulnerability on technical uphill finishes.
Final climb's variable gradient favored late accelerations over sustained attacks, VILELA caught despite leading through final corner.

rider_signals.CORENGO Eros: positive: converted poor pre-climb positioning into stage win using climbing prowess, demonstrated tactical maturity beyond young rider status
rider_signals.VILELA Andrea: negative: initiated winning move but lacked final 200m power after right-hand corner, punch insufficient despite Astana support
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: lost nearly all GC advantage on technical uphill finish, down to 6-second margin suggests weakness on steep punchy climbs
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: present in finale moves but dropped to 5th at 22 seconds, positioning adequate but finishing speed lacking
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: neutral: strong positioning through finale but beaten by pure climbers, confirming limits on steeper uphill sprints
